texto: In books I meet the dead as if they were alive,in books I see what is yet to come...All things decay and pass with time...all fame would fall victim to oblivionif God had not given mortal men the book to aid them.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Richard de Bury: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “In books I meet the dead as if they were alive,in books I see what is yet to come...All things de…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, books, love-of-books pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de love-of-books e the-philobiblon.

texto: [A]ll who are smitten with the love of books think cheaply of the world and wealth; as Jerome says to Vigilantius: The same man cannot love both gold and books... The hideousness of vice is greatly reprobated in books, so that he who loves to commune with books is lead to detest all manner of vice. The demon, who derives his name from knowledge, is most effectually defeated by the knowledge of books, and through books his multitudinous deceits and the endless labyrinths of his guile are laid bare to those who read...
explicacao: Citação de Richard de Bury sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “[A]ll who are smitten with the love of books think cheaply of the world and wealth; as Jerome say…” articula reflexao, books, reading em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como termino e reading.

texto: In books I find the dead as if they were alive; in books I foresee things to come; in books warlike affairs are set forth; from books come forth the laws of peace.All things are corrupted and decay in time; Saturn ceases not to devour the children that he generates; all the glory of the world would be buried in oblivion, unless God had provided mortals with the remedy of books.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Richard de Bury: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “In books I find the dead as if they were alive; in books I foresee things to come; in books warli…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, books, things pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
